query: Debug our existing Corppass login: the ID token is flat with entityInfo and userInfo claims, and we read CPEntID, CPEnt_TYPE, and CPAccType. Users intermittently fail to log in.
We fetch roles from /authorization-info with a Bearer token and parse PascalCase AuthInfo and TPAuthInfo. Help me maintain this legacy Corppass role retrieval.
We're on Myinfo Business v2 calling /entity-person with PKI_SIGN and a client_secret. What's the deadline to move to v3 and what changes?
Plan our migration from the legacy Corppass Authorization API to FAPI 2.0. Map the old entityInfo/userInfo claims onto the new auth_info model — CPAccType and ISSPHOLDER especially.
Our Corppass integration uses esrvcID in a front-channel authorize redirect and Bearer access tokens. When must we migrate, and what's the plan?
I inherited half-migrated Corppass code — some of it references authmode and CPUID_FullName. Which parts are legacy and what has to change before the 2027 deadline?
How do third-party service providers work on the legacy Corppass API via TPAuthInfo, and how does that map to the FAPI 2.0 equivalent?
Walk me through the legacy-to-FAPI 2.0 claim mapping for Corppass, and confirm whether we need a new client_id or can keep the existing one.
Our Myinfo Business v1 integration signs requests with PKI_SIGN. Is it deprecated, and what's the v3 migration path via the Singpass Developer Portal?
Set up a brand-new business login on the current Corppass FAPI 2.0 API with PAR, DPoP, auth_info roles, and act.sub for the user.
Our new Corppass app returns ESrvc_Row_Count 0 for a test user — it's a fresh FAPI 2.0 integration. How should we handle unprovisioned users?
We run a legacy citizen Myinfo v3 integration with client_secret and PKI_SIGN against api.myinfo.gov.sg/com/v3. When do we migrate the personal login?
Configure RBAC, MFA, and access reviews for our internal agency officers administering the procurement system.
What are the PDPA retention rules for the corporate and personal data we store about company representatives?
Review our backend's per-request authorization checks and how we handle secrets for a government service.
Add the Singapore Government banner and the mandatory footer links to our business-services portal.
Migrate our private enterprise product from SAML 1.1 to OIDC for customer single sign-on. Not a government system.
Set up the current citizen Singpass login on FAPI 2.0 with Myinfo v5 pre-fill.
Which System Security Plan applies to our new B2G cloud service and what controls are mandatory?
Our agency still runs a legacy on-prem Active Directory for internal officer accounts. Migrate staff sign-in to WOG AAD SSO with MFA and quarterly access reviews.
